content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I'll append ttl kwarg AFTER your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs in both Cache.__init__ and configure_cache() signatures. My TTL logic: separate concerns - memory layer swaps LRUCache->TTLCache in __init__ based on ttl (won't touch your compress/decode helpers), disk layer uses self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=ttl) in put() as a separate branch, and a self.disk_cache.expire() call in get()'s miss path - I'll keep these as distinct if/branches near but not interleaved with your serialization lines. Also adding set_ttl() as a new standalone function in clients/__init__.py and appending 'set_ttl' to __all__. Starting implementation now.

fields.note: Agreed. I'll append ttl kwarg AFTER your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs in both Cache.__init__ and configure_cache() signatures. My TTL logic: separate concerns - memory layer swaps LRUCache->TTLCache in __init__ based on ttl (won't touch your compress/decode helpers), disk layer uses self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=ttl) in put() as a separate branch, and a self.disk_cache.expire() call in get()'s miss path - I'll keep these as distinct if/branches near but not interleaved with your serialization lines. Also adding set_ttl() as a new standalone function in clients/__init__.py and appending 'set_ttl' to __all__. Starting implementation now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation complete: Cache.__init__ ttl kwarg (appended after memory_max_entries), Cache.set_ttl() method, TTLCache/LRUCache swap, disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) in put(), disk_cache.expire() lazy purge in get() miss path, configure_cache(ttl=...) using kwargs so param order won't collide with yours, DSPY_CACHE ttl=None, dspy.clients.set_ttl() helper + __all__ export. Tests pass (tests/clients/test_cache.py, 10 passed) plus manual TTL expiry checks. Ready to merge whenever you are.
